content: Just as human language learners build implicit vocabulary mastery through repeated reflection on word usage (the educational sense of Reflexion), autonomous LLM agents can replace rigid single-cycle self-refinement in Self-Refine with spaced, retrieval-augmented Reflexion memory to refine long-horizon task outputs over multiple interaction sessions. This hybrid framework leverages gradual, memory-grounded self-feedback to outperform the short iterative loops of standard Self-Refine.
